title: Land animals slaughtered for meat
subtitle: These numbers do not include additional deaths that happen during the production of meat and dairy, chickens slaughtered in the egg industry, and other land animals for which there is no data.
caption:
Land animals slaughtered for meat

These numbers do not include additional deaths that happen during the production of meat and dairy, chickens slaughtered in the egg industry, and other land animals for which there is no data.

This stacked area chart tracks the global number of land animals slaughtered for meat from 1961 to 2024, with the vertical axis measuring counts in billions up to a maximum of 100 billion. The visual data is dominated by a single category, chickens, represented by the large tan area that comprises the vast majority of the total volume. In 1961, the total count begins below 10 billion, with the chicken segment forming the bulk of this initial figure. Over the subsequent decades, the chart shows a continuous upward trajectory, crossing the 20 billion mark around 1980 and the 40 billion mark near the year 2000. By 2024, the total reaches approximately 90 billion, driven almost entirely by the expansion of the chicken category. Beneath this dominant layer, a cluster of other animal types forms a relatively thin, multi-colored band at the bottom of the chart. This group includes ducks, pigs, geese, sheep, rabbits, turkeys, goats, cattle, and other animals. While these categories show slight increases in thickness over the timeline, they remain compressed near the horizontal axis compared to the scale of poultry slaughter. The legend lists these species in descending order of their 2024 values, with ducks and pigs appearing as the most visible layers within this lower cluster, while cattle and other animals occupy the smallest visible bands at the very base.
